faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Simple answers to help you understand when guidance is useful and what information helps during the first contact.

items.a: Bioghaia works with landowners, rural producers, companies, construction projects, developments, and people who need to understand, regularize, permit, or plan an area with greater safety. You can start the conversation even if you are not sure which service you need yet.

items.q: When do I need environmental permitting?
items.a: Environmental permitting may be required for construction, productive activities, intervention in natural areas, land use, business expansion, area regularization, rural properties, or projects with potential environmental impact. An initial technical review helps identify whether your case requires a permit, authorization, technical study, CAR, or another procedure.

items.q: What is the difference between environmental permitting and environmental compliance?
items.a: Environmental permitting is usually related to authorization to install, operate, or expand an activity or development. Environmental compliance can involve correcting or organizing an existing situation, pending documents, land use, rural property requirements, CAR, or adaptation to legal requirements. In many cases, both topics appear together.

items.q: Do you work with rural areas and agriculture?
items.a: Yes. Bioghaia provides technical support for rural properties, crops, reforestation, land management, terrain analysis, CAR, and field problem identification. The goal is to reduce risk, organize information, and support safer decisions before investing or moving forward.

items.q: Does Bioghaia use drones and technology?
items.a: Yes. When the project requires greater precision, Bioghaia can use drones, sensors, aerial imagery, geospatial analysis, and territorial analysis to map areas, identify constraints, observe land characteristics, and generate more reliable technical information.

items.q: Do you serve littoral, mountain, and rural areas?
items.a: Yes. Bioghaia serves littoral, mountain, rural, urban, and expansion areas in Rio Grande do Sul. Each location may have different environmental characteristics and requirements, so a technical reading of the area is an important step.

items.q: I am not sure what I need. Can I still get in touch?
items.a: Yes. The initial conversation is designed to organize your situation, understand the type of project, identify the location, and suggest the most appropriate next step. You do not need to know technical terms or which permit to request.

items.q: How do I request a quote?
items.a: Send a WhatsApp message with the project location, a short description of what you intend to do, and, if possible, photos, documents, or information about the area. With these details, the initial guidance becomes clearer and the quote can be directed more accurately.
